summary: In a brief Q&A, billionaire entrepreneur Diane Hendricks shares her foundational beliefs on business, faith, and the American Dream. She emphasizes the importance of financial responsibility, honesty, and using a simple 'right vs. wrong' moral compass for decision-making. She also advises young entrepreneurs to take calculated risks, ensure their product is genuinely needed, and prioritize paying others before themselves.

principles.statement: Take financial responsibility as your number one priority.
principles.rationale: As an entrepreneur, you must ensure everyone else is paid before you are, and you must pay back financial partners to protect your reputation.
principles.source_quote: I personally take financial responsibility as the number one priority.

principles.statement: Base decisions on a simple test of right and wrong.
principles.rationale: Asking if something is the right thing to do naturally leads to considering whether it affects others positively or negatively.
principles.source_quote: Decisions are easier to make when you ask yourself this question. Is this the right thing to do?

principles.rationale: Lying creates a heavy burden on your soul that remains until you correct it and tell the truth.
principles.source_quote: Don’t lie—it will be a burden on your soul until you correct it and tell the truth.

mental_models.name: The Entrepreneur's Payment Hierarchy
mental_models.description: A lens for viewing business cash flow and responsibility where the founder is at the very bottom of the priority list for getting paid.
mental_models.example: When advising young entrepreneurs, she reminds them that 'everyone and everything gets paid before you. Many times you may not be able to pay yourself.'

heuristics: Make sure what you want to do or create is 'needed.'
Do not take more risk than you can afford to lose.
Everyone and everything gets paid before you.

signature_quotes.text: Don’t lie—it will be a burden on your soul until you correct it and tell the truth.
signature_quotes.context: When asked about a lesson learned early in life that led to her success.

signature_quotes.text: Take responsibility for your actions and keep in mind everyone and everything gets paid before you.
If you’re taking risks, do not take more risk than you can afford to lose. Do not let your dreams be at the expense of others.
Decisions are easier to make when you ask yourself this question. Is this the right thing to do?
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the influence of her father and the 10 commandments on her professional life.

anti_patterns.description: Taking on financial partners without a clear repayment plan.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It puts your future reputation at risk if you are unable to pay them back.

anti_patterns.description: Pursuing your entrepreneurial dreams at the expense of others.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It violates the fundamental rule of doing the 'right thing' and negatively affects others.
